mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull pressure that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative returns.
failure_condition: Joint high RSAFS and CPIAUCSL periods yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs rate effects, triggering recession fears and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline returns in a regime-filtered backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and improves risk appetite, driving immediate positive market repricing and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 5-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment alongside falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, supporting equity valuations and positive returns.
failure_condition: Combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease yields negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the joint signal shows no predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses corporate borrowing and equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase indicates weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in post-shock recovery periods yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over a 4-year sample.
